title: Boat Rental in Shannon River: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
Exploring Ireland's Shannon River on the water need not break the bank. With prices starting at around €150 for a half-day hire from Carrick-on-Shannon, renting a boat is an affordable way to see the river's historic castles and tranquil loughs. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners for a hassle-free booking experience.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Shannon River?

Rental costs start at €150 for a small motorboat for a half-day, rising to €800, €1,200 for a crewed yacht for a full day. Prices vary by boat size, season, and duration. For a comprehensive list of options and current rates, explore day charters in Shannon River on BoatHire24, where you can compare verified boat listings directly.

Where to set off in Shannon River

The Shannon River system offers numerous departure points. Key marinas include Carrick-on-Shannon Marina, Portumna Marina, and Athlone Marina. The villages of Leitrim Village and Banagher also have excellent facilities. Lough Derg and Lough Ree provide stunning cruising grounds, with Lough Derg being one of the largest lakes in Ireland. Many hire bases are located near these loughs, offering easy access to open water.

Which boat to rent

  1. Cruiser (e.g., Fairline 36), Ideal for families or groups of up to eight; from €400 for a full day.
  2. Motorboat (e.g., Axopar 28), Perfect for a day exploring narrow channels; seats six; from €250 for half a day.
  3. Catamaran (e.g., Lagoon 42), Spacious and stable for ten guests; from €900 for a crewed full day.
  4. Yacht (e.g., Sunseeker 50), Luxury experience with skipper, up to twelve; from €1,200 for a full day.

For a specific example, see the Salty Sun Charters charter page which details a 42-foot motor yacht based at Carrick-on-Shannon.

Cost breakdown: what to expect for your budget

  • Shared tour: €60, €90 per person for a 2-hour trip, often from Athlone.
  • Small motorboat half-day: €150, €250, suitable for up to six from Carrick-on-Shannon.
  • Catamaran full-day (self-drive): €500, €700, sleeping up to eight on Lough Derg.
  • Crewed yacht full-day: €800, €1,200, inclusive of skipper, for up to twelve guests.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Shannon River?

No licence is required for most rental boats in Ireland if you complete a short on-site briefing; the operator will confirm specific requirements. The Irish Inland Waterways Association provides guidelines, but hire firms usually handle training. High season runs from June to August, when south-westerly winds are common. For a smoother trip, consider spring or autumn.

Book early for July and August, as popular boats like the Axopar 28 are snapped up weeks in advance. A morning departure from Portumna gives you the best chance of calm water on Lough Derg.

At a glance

  • Price band: €60, €1,200 per person or per boat
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day or overnight
  • Capacity: 2, 12 guests per boat
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Shannon River

Can I rent a boat for a week on the Shannon?

Yes, many boats offer weekly hire from bases like Carrick-on-Shannon, with prices from €1,000 to €3,000, allowing you to explore Lough Ree and Lough Derg at your own pace.

Is it safe to navigate the Shannon River without experience?

Yes, provided you follow the operator's briefing. The river is well-marked with navigation buoys, and hire boats include safety equipment and charts.

What are the best stops along the Shannon?

Popular stops include the historic town of Athlone, Banagher with its castle, and the pretty village of Leitrim. Each offers mooring, pubs, and shops.

Can I bring my own food and drink on board?

Yes, most hire boats allow you to bring your own provisions. Some cruisers have small galleys with cooking facilities.

Final thoughts

Renting a boat in Shannon River for a day or longer is an affordable and memorable way to explore Ireland's waterways. With prices from €150 and options ranging from half-day trips to week-long adventures, there is something for every budget. Boat owners can also list their vessels on the BoatHire24 marketplace to reach travellers worldwide.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Shannon River and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
